Solano Home-Visiting Community Advisory Board logo
Vision & Mission:
Vision: Solano County’s children are healthy, safe & thriving, our families resilient, strong and vibrant
Mission: The CAB aligns, integrates and expands quality home visiting programs to produce healthy outcomes for expectant and parenting families in Solano County.
Overview:
The CAB members represent home-visiting programs serving children 0-5 years old throughout Solano County; county departments; community-based social services and health services agencies; funding entities; and school districts. Representatives include health professionals, school health personnel and selected staff members of represented home visiting programs, such as the program manager, supervisor or home visitors. In addition, the CAB shall include parents of children (0-5 years old) who are current or previous participants of the home visiting programs. Members aim to actively engage and retain participants by:
Creating enduring partnerships
Developing sustainable funding
Facilitating awareness of home visiting programs and community services
Fostering linkages to additional services and resources (i.e., client support)
Generating and sustaining referrals
Improving coordination and collaboration among service providers
Promoting culturally sensitive practices and core competencies
Meeting Schedule:
The Community Advisory Board meets four (4) times per year, usually on the first (1
st
) Wednesday of the months of:
January
April
July
October
